narrative:
The market sits comfortably in a business as usual regime, with the stress score at 11 out of 100 registering as genuinely calm rather than artificially suppressed. Equities have drifted modestly higher over the past five days, with SPY returning 1.2 percent at a z-score well within one standard deviation, confirming that this is an orderly, low-conviction grind rather than a momentum surge.

The VIX at 15.8 sits at the 26th percentile of its ten-year history, and the term structure is in normal contango, signalling that options markets see no near-term event risk worth pricing. Credit is reinforcing that message emphatically: HY spreads at 271 basis points represent the 7th percentile of their five-year range, and IG at 75 basis points carries a z-score of negative 1.14, meaning both markets are historically tight and fully corroborating the benign equity environment.

The three defensive sectors, utilities, real estate, and consumer staples, are the only red patches in the sector map, losing between 2.2 and 3.2 percent over five days while technology surged 7.1 percent. This rotation out of rate-sensitive defensives and into growth reflects a market repricing duration risk rather than expressing fear, and the strength in EEM and FXI adding 3.5 and 1.7 percent respectively suggests that risk appetite extends beyond domestic equities into emerging markets.
